item.title: BREAKING: Playgrounds in Hastings to get £1.5 MILLION after MP’s Campaign
item.description: Local MP Helena Dollimore is celebrating a £1.5 million investment to Hastings playgrounds, following her campaign with local children and parents to improve play spaces for local children. Today it has been announced that playgrounds across the town will benefit from major upgrades, including a significant refurbishment of the playground in Alexandra Park, thanks to a major investment from the Labour Government. The investment comes after Helena launched a campaign calling on councils,...
item.dc:creator: 1066helena

item.title: Helena Dollimore MP Launches campaign to improve playgrounds
item.description: Helena Dollimore MP has launched a new campaign to improve local playgrounds for children across Hastings, Rye and the villages. The Labour and Co-operative MP has carried out an audit of all the playgrounds in her constituency and found that 8 playgrounds had closed since 2014 and over half of the playgrounds in Hastings, Rye and the villages needed upgrading . As a result, too many families in Hastings, Rye and the villages do not have an adequate playground within walking distance from...
item.dc:creator: 1066helena

item.title: Update on cardiology services at Hastings and Eastbourne hospitals
item.description: Improving our local NHS is a key priority for me as your Member of Parliament. I am pleased that NHS waiting lists are continuing to fall...
item.dc:creator: 1066helena

item.title: Schools Summit with 20 local headteachers
item.description: This week I hosted a Schools Summit, bringing 20 local headteachers around the table at East Sussex College in Hastings with officials...
item.dc:creator: 1066helena

item.title: How should we spend our £20 million Pride in Place fund to improve Hastings?
item.description: Hastings is one of 75 towns to be awarded £20m in funding as part of the Labour Government's Pride in Place. This Labour Government has rightly said that the money should be spend on the priorities of local people, whether that is broken pavements, broken bus shelters, trying to open up important community venues like St Mary in the Castle, fixing up football facilities, or something else. We have seen previous funding schemes sometimes go wrong, such as the money sadly wasted on the...
item.dc:creator: 1066helena
